description: Pre-flight checks for Windows Server 2016 to 2019 in-place upgrade. This document only performs checks and never modifies the instance.

AutomationAssumeRole.description: (Optional) IAM role ARN the automation assumes - select WindowsUpgradeAutomationRole (created per iam-roles.md). If left empty, the run uses the invoking user's identity.

inputs.Script:
import boto3

def script_handler(events, context):
  instance_id = events['InstanceId']
  region = events['Region']
  ec2 = boto3.client('ec2', region_name=region)
  
  response = ec2.describe_instances(InstanceIds=[instance_id])
  instance = response['Reservations'][0]['Instances'][0]
  instance_type = instance['InstanceType']
  
  warnings = []; notes = []
  
  # Check instance store
  instance_store_devices = [bdm['DeviceName'] for bdm in instance.get('BlockDeviceMappings', []) if 'Ebs' not in bdm]
  type_info = ec2.describe_instance_types(InstanceTypes=[instance_type])['InstanceTypes'][0]
  has_instance_store = type_info.get('InstanceStorageSupported', False)
  
  if instance_store_devices or has_instance_store:
    devices_str = ', '.join(instance_store_devices) if instance_store_devices else 'ephemeral storage available'
    notes.append(f'INFO: Instance store ({devices_str}) present - preserved across the upgrade (reboot only, no stop/start)')
  
  # Check public IP
  public_ip = instance.get('PublicIpAddress')
  if public_ip:
    eip_response = ec2.describe_addresses(Filters=[{'Name': 'public-ip', 'Values': [public_ip]}])
    if not eip_response['Addresses']:
      notes.append(f'INFO: Dynamic public IP ({public_ip}) is retained across the upgrade (reboot only; changes only on stop/start)')
  
  summary = [f'Instance ID: {instance_id}', f'Instance Type: {instance_type}', f'AZ: {instance["Placement"]["AvailabilityZone"]}']
  if public_ip:
    summary.append(f'Public IP: {public_ip}')
  summary.append('')
  summary.extend(notes); summary.extend(warnings if warnings else ['No warnings'])
  
  result = '\n'.join(summary)
  print(result)
  return {'Summary': result, 'HasWarnings': 'true' if warnings else 'false'}

commands: if ($os.Caption -notmatch 'Windows Server 2016') { throw 'ERROR: Not Windows Server 2016. Found: ' + $os.Caption }
if ($ssm.Status -ne 'Running') { throw 'ERROR: SSM Agent not running' }
if (Test-Path 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Component Based Servicing\RebootPending') { throw 'ERROR: Reboot pending' }
if ($ec2Config) { throw 'ERROR: EC2Config detected. Migrate to EC2Launch before upgrading.' }
throw "ERROR: Insufficient disk space ($freeGB GB) and C: is not the last partition"
throw 'ERROR: MBR disk at 2TB limit with insufficient space'

inputs.Script:
def script_handler(events, context):
  print('=== PRE-CHECK COMPLETE ===')
  print('')
  print(events['Summary'])
  print('')
  print(f"Role: {events['RoleMessage']}")
  print('')
  print(events['OSChecks'])
  print('')
  os_lang = '1033'
  for line in events['OSChecks'].split('\n'):
    if line.startswith('OSLANGUAGE:'):
      os_lang = line.split(':')[1].strip()
  supported = ['1028','1029','1031','1033','1034','1036','1038','1040','1041','1042','1043','1045','1046','1049','1053','1055','2070','3082']
  if os_lang not in supported:
    raise Exception(f'INCOMPATIBLE: OS language LCID {os_lang} is not supported for in-place upgrade. Supported LCIDs: {supported}')
  print('All checks passed.')
  return {'Status': 'Complete', 'OSChecksRan': 'true', 'OSLanguage': os_lang}

def script_handler(events, context):
  print('=== PRE-CHECK COMPLETE (PARTIAL) ===')
  print('')
  print(events['Summary'])
  print('')
  print(f"Role: {events['RoleMessage']}")
  print('')
  print('NOTE: Unable to run OS checks - instance lacks SSM permissions.')
  print('OS checks include: Windows version, pending reboot, EC2Config, disk space/layout.')
  print('')
  print('API-based checks passed. Attach an SSM-enabled role to run full checks.')
  return {'Status': 'PartialComplete', 'OSChecksRan': 'false', 'OSLanguage': '1033'}
